title: eData - A data entry, formatting, and review tool for exposure data
text: app designed guide users formatting, cleaning annotation exposure/pollution/monitoring data (e.g. mg/L chemical environmental matrix). Published studies reports important source data, often fragmented difficult analyse without extensive data cleaning transformation. assisting automating step, hope make exposure assessment - therefore risk assessment chemicals environment - easy possible. app part Source Outcome Pathway/Risk assessment database family R Shiny apps, provides one-half data necessary environmental risk assessment. counterpart toxicity/bioassay data STOP qData. Environmental risk predictions can viewed Source Outcome Predictor. (back top)

title: eData - A data entry, formatting, and review tool for exposure data

Download package devtools/pak R   devtools::install_github("NIVANorge/STOPeData")   # pak::pak("NIVANorge/STOPeData") Install missing R packages (Optional) Enter API keys/tokens .Renviron file Change git remote url avoid accidental pushes base project Run app locally: (back top)

title: eData - A data entry, formatting, and review tool for exposure data

title: CREED Relevance Criteria Configuration — CREED_relevance_criteria_config
text: Returns configuration list 11 CREED relevance criteria (RV1-RV11). criterion includes title whether Required Recommended.

title: CREED Relevance Criteria Configuration — CREED_relevance_criteria_config
CREED Relevance Criteria Configuration — CREED_relevance_criteria_config
text: named list element criterion configuration : title Character. Human-readable criterion title. type Character. Either "Required" "Recommended".

title: CREED Relevance Criteria Configuration — CREED_relevance_criteria_config
text: Relevance criteria assess fitness--purpose data: - RV1-RV2: Media (sample medium, collection method) - RV3-RV4: Spatial (study area, site type) - RV5-RV7: Temporal (timespan, frequency, conditions) - RV8-RV9: Analytical (analyte, sensitivity) - RV10: Data handling (summary statistics) - RV11: Supporting parameters

title: CREED Relevance Criteria Configuration — CREED_relevance_criteria_config
CREED Reliability Criteria Configuration — CREED_reliability_criteria_config
text: Returns configuration list 19 CREED reliability criteria (RB1-RB19). criterion includes title whether Required Recommended.

title: CREED Reliability Criteria Configuration — CREED_reliability_criteria_config
CREED Reliability Criteria Configuration — CREED_reliability_criteria_config
text: named list element criterion configuration : title Character. Human-readable criterion title. type Character. Either "Required" "Recommended".

title: CREED Reliability Criteria Configuration — CREED_reliability_criteria_config
text: Reliability criteria assess methodological quality data: - RB1-RB3: Media (sample medium, collection method, handling) - RB4: Spatial (site location) - RB5: Temporal (date time) - RB6-RB13: Analytical (analytes, LOD/LOQ, QA/QC, methods) - RB14-RB18: Data handling (calculations, significant figures, outliers, censored data) - RB19: Supporting parameters RB8 (Accreditation/QMS) shortcut criterion: fully met, RB9-RB12 may skipped.

title: CREED Reliability Criteria Configuration — CREED_reliability_criteria_config

Generate Protocol ID —- — generate_protocol_id
text: Generates standardised protocol identifier based protocol type, name, sequence number, campaign. function fully vectorised can handle multiple protocols simultaneously.

title: Generate Protocol ID —- — generate_protocol_id
Generate Protocol ID —- — generate_protocol_id
text: protocol_type Character vector. category protocol (e.g., "Sampling Protocol", "Analytical Protocol"). Must match one predefined categories default "X". protocol_name Character vector. specific name/method within protocol category. abbreviated cleaned ID generation. sequence_number Numeric vector. Sequential number protocols within category. Defaults 1. zero-padded 2 digits. campaign_name Character vector. Name campaign/study. abbreviated first 10 alphanumeric characters. Defaults "".

title: Generate Protocol ID —- — generate_protocol_id
text: Character vector generated protocol identifiers, one per input element,   formatted TypeCode + SequenceNumber + AbbreviatedName + CampaignAbbrev.   :   - TypeCode: S (Sampling), F (Fractionation), E (Extraction),     (Analytical), X (Unknown)   - SequenceNumber: Zero-padded 2-digit number   - AbbreviatedName: 15 alphanumeric characters (optional)   - CampaignAbbrev: 10 alphanumeric characters (optional)

title: Generate Protocol ID —- — generate_protocol_id
text: function handles edge cases gracefully: - NULL empty values result appropriate defaults - Invalid protocol types default "X" - Names cleaned special characters spaces - Empty components omitted final ID

title: Generate Protocol ID —- — generate_protocol_id
code: generate_protocol_id("Sampling Protocol", "Water grab sampling", sequence_number = 1) #> [1] "S01_WaterGrabSampli" generate_protocol_id("Analytical Protocol", "ICP-MS", sequence_number = 2, campaign_name = "NorthSea2022") #> [1] "A02_Icpms_NorthSea20"

what: Generate a Zenodo submission README — generate_zenodo_readme
title: Generate a Zenodo submission README — generate_zenodo_readme
text: Produces markdown-formatted README string suitable   attaching Zenodo record. Used companion file uploaded   dataset preview shown production confirmation   modal.

title: Generate a Zenodo submission README — generate_zenodo_readme
code: generate_zenodo_readme(   title,   description,   authors,   license_id,   contact_name = "",   contact_email = "" )

title: Generate a Zenodo submission README — generate_zenodo_readme
text: title Character. Dataset title. description Character. Dataset description. authors List lists, elements `first`, `last`, `affiliation`, `orcid` (character, may empty strings). license_id Character. Zenodo license identifier, e.g. `"cc--4.0"`. human-readable title looked `zenodo_licenses`. contact_name Character. Display name role contact. contact_email Character. Contact email address.

title: Generate a Zenodo submission README — generate_zenodo_readme
text: Character string containing full README markdown format.

title: Generate a Zenodo submission README — generate_zenodo_readme
code: readme <- generate_zenodo_readme(   title = "Marine contaminant monitoring 2020-2022",   description = "Trace metal concentrations in blue mussels from Norwegian fjords.",   authors = list(     list(first = "Jane", last = "Smith", affiliation = "NIVA", orcid = "")   ),   license_id = "cc-by-4.0",   contact_name = "Jane Smith",   contact_email = "jane.smith@niva.no" ) cat(readme) #> # Marine contaminant monitoring 2020-2022 #> - **Zenodo DOI:** (Will be generated upon upload) #>  #> --- #>  #> ## What is this? #>  #> Trace metal concentrations in blue mussels from Norwegian fjords. #>  #> --- #>  #> ## Authors #>  #> **Primary Author:** Jane Smith #>   **Institution:** NIVA #>  #> --- #>  #> ## How to cite #>  #> Smith J. (2026). *Marine contaminant monitoring 2020-2022*. Zenodo. https://doi.org/[DOI-will-be-generated] #>  #> --- #>  #> ## License #>  #> Released under **Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International**. #>  #> --- #>  #> ## Contact #>  #> - **Name:** Jane Smith #> - **Email:** jane.smith@niva.no #> - **Institution:** NIVA

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   bibtex_str <- "@article{smith2022,     author  = {Smith, Jane},     title   = {Marine pollution},     journal = {Marine Pollution Bulletin},     year    = {2022}   }"   df <- bib_string2df_alt(bibtex_str)   fields <- map_bibtex_to_reference_fields(df)   fields$TITLE } # }

what: Map Crossref data to reference input fields — map_crossref_to_reference_fields
title: Map Crossref data to reference input fields — map_crossref_to_reference_fields
text: Converts Crossref API response data list values correspond reference module input fields.

title: Map Crossref data to reference input fields — map_crossref_to_reference_fields
Map Crossref data to reference input fields — map_crossref_to_reference_fields
text: crossref_data Data frame returned rcrossref::cr_works() access_date Date use ACCESS_DATE field. Defaults today's date.

title: Map Crossref data to reference input fields — map_crossref_to_reference_fields
text: named list containing mapped field values reference input fields.   Values NA fields present Crossref data.

title: Map Crossref data to reference input fields — map_crossref_to_reference_fields
text: Maps Crossref fields reference fields: - type → REFERENCE_TYPE (journal-article → journal, book → book, etc.) - title → TITLE - author → AUTHOR (formatted Last, First; Last, First) - published.online/published.print → YEAR - container.title → PERIODICAL_JOURNAL - volume → VOLUME - issue → ISSUE - publisher → PUBLISHER - DOI → DOI - URL → URL - page → PAGES - ISBN/ISSN → ISBN_ISSN

title: Map Crossref data to reference input fields — map_crossref_to_reference_fields

title: Reference field character limits — reference_character_limits
text: Provides named list character limits reference metadata fields ensure data quality database compatibility. Limits include: DOCUMENT_NUMBER: 200 characters DOI: 200 characters EDITION: 200 characters INSTITUTION: 200 characters ISBN_ISSN: 200 characters PERIODICAL_JOURNAL: 200 characters PUBLISHER: 200 characters REF_COMMENT: 1000 characters URL: 200 characters

Run the Shiny Application — run_app
text: Run Shiny Application

title: Run the Shiny Application — run_app
code: run_app(   onStart = NULL,   options = list(),   enableBookmarking = "server",   uiPattern = "/",   ... )

title: Run the Shiny Application — run_app
text: onStart function called app actually run. needed shinyAppObj, since shinyAppDir case, global.R file can used purpose. options Named options passed runApp call (can following: "port", "launch.browser", "host", "quiet", "display.mode" "test.mode"). can also specify width height parameters provide hint embedding environment ideal height/width app. enableBookmarking Can one "url", "server", "disable". default value, NULL, respect setting previous calls  enableBookmarking(). See enableBookmarking() information bookmarking app. uiPattern regular expression applied GET request determine whether ui used handle request. Note entire request path must match regular expression order match considered successful. ... arguments pass golem_opts. See `?golem::get_golem_options` details.

title: Run the Shiny Application — run_app

title: Summarise biota data — summarise_biota
code: biota <- data.frame(   SPECIES_GROUP = c("Fish", "Fish"),   SAMPLE_SPECIES = c("Gadus morhua", "Clupea harengus"),   SAMPLE_TISSUE = c("Liver", "Muscle"),   SAMPLE_SPECIES_LIFESTAGE = c("Adult", "Juvenile"),   SAMPLE_SPECIES_GENDER = c("Male", "Female") ) summarise_biota(biota, SAMPLE_SPECIES = TRUE, SAMPLE_TISSUE = TRUE) #> [1] "2 biota samples. Species (2): Gadus morhua, Clupea harengus. Tissue types (2): Liver, Muscle"

title: Validate dataset structure —- — validate_dataset_structure
code: df <- data.frame(SITE_CODE = c("S001", "S002"), LATITUDE = c(59.1, 57.4)) validate_dataset_structure(df, "Sites") #> $valid #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $message #> [1] "Valid structure" #>  validate_dataset_structure(data.frame(), "Sites") #> $valid #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $message #> [1] "Data has no columns" #>

title: Validate DOI format — validate_doi_format
text: Validates whether string contains valid DOI format. Accepts DOIs without URL prefixes (https://doi.org/ http://dx.doi.org/).

text: input_string Character string validate DOI

text: Logical indicating whether input valid DOI format

title: Validate DOI format — validate_doi_format
text: DOI format follows pattern: 10.registrant/suffix registrant typically 4+ digits suffix can contain various characters. function validates format , whether DOI actually exists.

title: Write CREED report as human-readable text file — write_creed_report_txt

title: Zenodo license options tibble — zenodo_licenses
text: Returns tibble Zenodo license options March 2026. Includes `id`, `title`, `description`, `url`, `schema`, `osi_approved`, `revision_id`, `created`, `updated`, `popular`, custom boolean highlighting commonly used licenses.

title: Zenodo license options tibble — zenodo_licenses
Zenodo license options tibble — zenodo_licenses
text: object class tbl_df (inherits tbl, data.frame) 444 rows 10 columns.

title: Zenodo license options tibble — zenodo_licenses
code: # Show only popular licenses zenodo_licenses[zenodo_licenses$popular, c("id", "title")] #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   id              title                                                          #>   <chr>           <chr>                                                          #> 1 apache-2.0      Apache License 2.0                                             #> 2 cc-by-4.0       Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International                 #> 3 cc-by-nc-4.0    Creative Commons Attribution Non Commercial 4.0 International  #> 4 cc-by-nc-sa-4.0 Creative Commons Attribution Non Commercial Share Alike 4.0 I… #> 5 cc-by-sa-4.0    Creative Commons Attribution Share Alike 4.0 International     #> 6 mit             MIT License

what: Zenodo resource type options vector — zenodo_resource_types
title: Zenodo resource type options vector — zenodo_resource_types
text: Named character vector Zenodo resource types.   Names display labels; values Zenodo resource type identifiers.   See https://help.zenodo.org/docs/deposit/describe-records/resource-type/   details. Filtered values likely used application.

title: Zenodo resource type options vector — zenodo_resource_types
Zenodo resource type options vector — zenodo_resource_types
text: object class character length 5.
